metadata.seed: My partner was seeing someone else on the side

patient.query: I guess part of it is that I thought we had something special, you know? It feels like I invested so much of myself into it, and now I just question everything. Did I misread things? Was it ever really what I thought? It's like I don't know who I am without them anymore, and that’s really unsettling.

patient.query: Yeah, I thought we connected on a deeper level. There were moments when we just got each other, you know? Conversations that felt endless, laughter that felt so genuine. It was like we understood each other's quirks and flaws. But now, looking back, I wonder if it was all just in my head. Maybe I was just clinging to the good times, and I ignored the signs. It’s really confusing.

patient.query: I don't know, there were little things—maybe arguments that felt bigger than they should have been or times when we didn’t communicate like we used to. I brushed them off as normal relationship stuff, but now I wonder if I was just trying to convince myself that everything was fine. It’s like I was in this bubble, and now that it’s popped, I see the cracks I didn’t notice before. It makes me feel foolish, like I should have known better.

patient.query: Yeah, I think part of me just wanted to believe it was all perfect. I didn’t want to rock the boat or make things awkward. There was this fear of losing what we had, so I kept telling myself that everything would be okay if I just… ignored the issues. I guess I thought love would be enough to fix everything, but now it just feels like I was fooling myself.

fear of losing what we had

love would be enough to fix everything
I was fooling myself

patient.query: I think I was just really afraid of being alone, you know? Losing them felt scarier than facing the problems. I didn’t want to risk it all for a chance at something better. It was like I convinced myself that keeping the peace was the right choice, even if it hurt deep down. Now that it's all over, it feels like I just prolonged the inevitable. It's frustrating to think about.

patient.query: Being alone makes me feel... vulnerable, I guess. It’s like a huge part of my identity was wrapped up in that relationship. Now, the quiet feels overwhelming. I worry about what people will think, or that maybe I’m not enough on my own. There’s this loneliness that creeps in, and it makes everything feel heavier. I just don’t want to feel abandoned or like I’m not worthy of love.

automatic_thoughts: I worry about what people will think
I'm not enough on my own
I feel abandoned
I'm not worthy of love

Okay, it sounds like being alone is bringing up a lot for you. You've described feeling vulnerable, overwhelmed by the quiet, and worried about what others think, and a deep sense of loneliness. It's clear that this relationship was a significant part of your sense of self, and its absence is leaving you feeling quite exposed. You've also expressed a fear of abandonment and feeling unworthy of love. That's a lot to carry.

It takes courage to acknowledge these feelings. Can you tell me a little more about what it means to you to feel "not enough" on your own?
